text.title: Neo4j Graph Database Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real nosql-neo4j-patterns deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: User.friends_list = [user_id_1, user_id_2, ...]; pointer traversal broken; back to relational join.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated Neo4j like Postgres + JSON columns.
antipattern.fix: Put context (date, weight, type) on the relationship; nodes carry entity properties only.

antipattern.symptom: MATCH (u)-[:FRIEND*]->(f) on a power-law graph explodes to millions of paths before LIMIT applies.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote *recursive* traversal without depth cap.
antipattern.fix: Cap with *..N + LIMIT; PROFILE on a representative sample before deploying.

antipattern.symptom: WHERE u.email = '...' on 10M nodes; query iterates every User; latency seconds.
antipattern.fix: CREATE INDEX on every filtered property; CREATE CONSTRAINT for globally unique fields.
